message:
So last week my kids complained they were bored—same old screen time, same old everything. I dug out some old board games from the closet, and we spent the whole evening playing by lamplight, actually *talking* to each other. No phones. No distractions.

It got me thinking: when was the last time we actually *practiced* our fire escape plan together? Not as a chore, but just... talking through it? Turns out, a calm conversation about "if we heard the smoke alarm, where would we go?" is way less scary than I expected—kind of turned it into a little family moment.

Emergency prep doesn't have to feel heavy. Sometimes it's just the good stuff: being together, knowing what to do, and feeling a little more solid about things.

Have you gone over an escape plan with your family? How did you make it feel natural instead of stressful?
